description: Re-derivation machinery arc (DEC-0036, step 2): build .context clean via the config-based framework — edge-based validator (units 2.1/2.1b=TASK-045), packaged conception (2.2=TASK-046), rename engine (2.3=TASK-044); remaining units 2.4-2.7 + re-populate .context (step 3) + extension refactor (step 4) + cutover (step 5). SUPERSEDES the original in-place inline-FK migration (abandoned per DEC-0036): the DEC-0013 clean-edge goal is achieved by construction in .context, not by stripping .project.
acceptance_criteria: config.relation_types[] declares all relation_type canonical_ids per DEC-0030 tripartite mapping
.project/relations.json carries one edge per former inline FK reference
inline FK fields removed/deprecated from schemas with version bump + drop-field MigrationFn registered
validateProject clean: no dangling edge refs, no unregistered relation_types
non-substrate-target fields (issues.resolved_by / spec-reviews.resolved_by commit strings) NOT auto-migrated; surfaced for user decision
tasks.phase free-text migration gated on DEC-0028 (phase shape) — STOP + surface if unresolved
notes: REFRAMED per DEC-0036 (2026-05-17): in-place inline-FK migration of .project superseded by re-derivation. FGAP-046 closure now = author .context references as relations.json edges (conform by construction), not strip .project in place. The in-place attempt hit cascading schema-invariant collisions (tasks completed→VER if-then; decisions ≥1-forcing-artifact anyOf); tmp/migrate-phase2.ts discardable. This work folds into .context re-population (DEC-0036 finishing step 3). Stays in-progress as the inline-FK-elimination concern, now realized via canonical authoring.
